title: Sinners Makeup Designer on Vampire Effects
text: Mike Fontaine, nominated for Best Makeup and Hairstyling alongside Ken Diaz and Shunika Terry, created the vampire effects for Ryan Coogler's Sinners by drawing inspiration from nature and real-world references. Fontaine studied animal attacks on humans, examining medical photos of injuries from bears, apes, and dogs, explaining: We turned up the volume a bit, but everything was still rooted in reality. The vampire eyes utilized tapetum lucidum, the reflective layer found in nocturnal animals' eyes, which Coogler incorporated into the script. Contact lens designer Cristina Patterson developed custom reflective lenses specifically for this production. Fontaine noted: It's rare when somebody invents something brand-new. It's a mesmerizing effect. These lenses, which caused slight vision impairment during filming, created an effect that was simultaneously terrifying and beautiful. Fontaine praised the cast's professionalism, particularly Jack O'Connell, who endured extensive prosthetics including dentures, elongated fingers, contacts, and a metal plate during filming in New Orleans.
